breakdown: The PR makes a focused refactor replacing deprecated timezone.utc with UTC in datetime calls. Naming is good (no new generic names introduced). Error handling unchanged (not applicable for this API migration). Type safety is solid (UTC is the recommended singleton). Architecture is clean - localized changes, no module boundary changes. Test coverage cannot be assessed from diff alone - testing_mcp changes don't appear to include test modifications. Documentation includes a large design doc section but most appears to be planning rather than code docs.
key_changes: Replace datetime.now(timezone.utc) with datetime.now(UTC) in base_test.py (9 occurrences)
Replace datetime.fromtimestamp(..., tz=timezone.utc) with datetime.fromtimestamp(..., tz=UTC) in base_test.py (2 occurrences)
Remove timezone import from datetime, add UTC import
Large design document section discussing various issues (not code changes)
